Hi there! Let me know if you need any help with the lab or have questions about the task.

A VM, or virtual machine, is basically a software-based computer that runs inside a physical computer. It behaves like a real computer with its own operating system and apps, but it’s all virtualized—meaning it shares the physical hardware with other VMs. This lets you run multiple “computers” on one physical machine, which is great for testing, development, or running different environments without needing separate hardware.
